document.title: EN 62220-1-1 Test Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Test report for A1717MCW, A1417MCW and F1417MCW digital flat panel X-ray detectors covering EN 62220-1-1 MTF and DQE measurements.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical documentation by documenting physical, operational, functional, exposure, safety and test-result characteristics for the listed digital flat panel X-ray detector models.
keyPoints: The report states that it was provided for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices and includes MTF and DQE measurements tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015).
A1717MCW, A1417MCW and F1417MCW are described as Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector accessories with the same performance and usage except for size.
The devices use a CsI scintillator to convert x-ray energy to light and photodiode arrays to convert light to electrical signals.
The devices are not for fluoroscopic use and are fixed at 1 fps because they are static x-ray detectors.
The report lists DQE, MTF, NPS, linearity, lag, defect, stability and uniformity measurements for the three models.

evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-5-116 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: A1717MCW, A1417MCW and F1417MCW have the same performance and usage except for size.
evidence.evidenceText: Performance and usage are all the same expect for size. (A-TCF-5-127)

evidence.claim: The applicable standard is EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 for determination of detective quantum efficiency of detectors used in radiographic imaging.
evidence.evidenceText: 12-289 EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 Medical electrical equipment-Characteristics of digital X-ray imaging devices Part 1-1: Determination of the detective quantum efficiency Detectors used in radiographic imaging.

evidence.claim: A1717MCW has entire dimensions of 460 mm x 460 mm and a panel size of 430 mm x 430 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: A1717MCW Entire dimensions include outer short ring 460 (X) mm x 460 (Y) mm Panel size (outer short ring removed) 430 mm x 430 mm

evidence.claim: A1417MCW has entire dimensions of 384 mm x 460 mm and a panel size of 358.4 mm x 430 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: A1417MCW Entire dimensions include outer short ring 384 (X) mm x 460 (Y) mm Panel size (outer short ring removed) 358.4 mm x 430 mm

evidence.claim: F1417MCW has entire dimensions of 384 mm x 460 mm and a panel size of 358.4 mm x 430 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: F1417MCW Entire dimensions include outer short ring 384 (X) mm x 460 (Y) mm Panel size (outer short ring removed) 358.4 mm x 430 mm

evidence.claim: A1717MCW has an active pixel area size of 425.04 mm x 425.6 mm and 3,036 x 3,040 active area pixels.
evidence.evidenceText: A1717MCW Active pixel area size 425.04 mm x 425.6 mm Number of active area pixel 3,036 x 3,040 = 9,229,440

evidence.claim: A1417MCW and F1417MCW each have an active pixel area size of 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm and 2,466 x 3,040 active area pixels.
evidence.evidenceText: A1417MCW Active pixel area size 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm Number of active area pixel 2,466 x 3,040 = 7,496,640 ... F1417MCW Active pixel area size 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm Number of active area pixel 2,466 x 3,040 = 7,496,640

evidence.claim: The detector uses CsI as the scintillator.
evidence.evidenceText: Part Contents Scintillator CsI Converting X-ray to Visible light to charge Phot-Diode

evidence.claim: The detector frame rate is 1 fps.
evidence.evidenceText: Frame Rate 1 fps

evidence.claim: A1717MCW, A1417MCW and F1417MCW generate a digital x-ray image in response to a trigger signal but do not initiate an X-ray generator or control patient exposure settings.
evidence.evidenceText: A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW generates a digital x-ray image in response to a trigger signal, but does not initiate an X-ray generator or control the x-ray exposure setting for a patient.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Operation

evidence.claim: The device uses a CsI screen on a substrate as the x-ray absorber.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW device uses a CsI screen on a substrate.

evidence.claim: X-ray photons are converted to light in the screen, and photodiode arrays convert the light to electrical signals.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray photons interacting in the screen are converted directly to light. This light exits from the screen and be absorbed at photodiode arrays. The photodiode arrays convert light to electrical signals proportional to the intensity of the light.

evidence.claim: The detector outputs raw image data to the workstation via 1Gbps Ethernet or wireless to an access point.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector outputs raw image data to the workstation, via 1Gbps Ethernet or Wireless to Access Point.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Readout

evidence.claim: Acquisition software receives 16-bit digital image data and stores it in 16-bit format.
evidence.evidenceText: The acquisition software in the workstation receives the 16-bit digital image data and processes it. It is stored in the hard disk in a 16-bit format.

evidence.claim: Saved raw data from the detectors is transferred to end-user software and converted to DICOM or Bitmap.
evidence.evidenceText: The saved raw data from A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW will be transferred to the end user’s S/W and then converted to a DICOM or Bitmap file.

evidence.claim: All image data are transmitted and stored as 16-bit little-endian two-dimensional arrays without a specific header.
evidence.evidenceText: All image data shall be transmitted and stored as 16-bits little-endian (Intel bytes order) two- dimensional arrays without any specific header.

evidence.claim: The device is not for fluoroscopic use.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not for fluoroscopic.

evidence.claim: DQE criteria for A1717MCW, A1417MCW and F1417MCW are 0.1 lp/mm at at least 70%, 0.5 lp/mm at at least 62%, 1.0 lp/mm at at least 53%, 2.0 lp/mm at at least 42%, and 3.0 lp/mm at at least 19%.
Measured DQE values include A1717MCW 79% at 0.1 lp/mm, 69% at 0.5 lp/mm, 62% at 1.0 lp/mm, 49% at 2.0 lp/mm and 31% at 3.0 lp/mm.
Measured DQE values include A1417MCW 78% at 0.1 lp/mm, 69% at 0.5 lp/mm, 61% at 1.0 lp/mm, 50% at 2.0 lp/mm and 34% at 3.0 lp/mm.
Measured DQE values include F1417MCW 76% at 0.1 lp/mm, 71% at 0.5 lp/mm, 64% at 1.0 lp/mm, 51% at 2.0 lp/mm and 35% at 3.0 lp/mm.
The detector element pitch is 0.14 mm, corresponding to a sampling cut-off frequency of 3.57 lp/mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Its pitch of this system is 0.14 mm corresponding to a sampling cut-off frequency of 3.57 lp/mm.

evidence.claim: Artifacts due to aliasing are stated to be negligible, and no evidence of aliasing was observed in a test pattern image.
evidence.evidenceText: Therefore, artifacts in the image due to aliasing will be negligible. In addition, no evidence of aliasing was observed in a test pattern image acquired with this detector

evidence.claim: A1717MCW linearity was measured between 0.34 and 127.10 μGy.
evidence.evidenceText: A1717MCW * The linearity of detector was measured between 0.34 and 127.10μGy.

evidence.claim: A1417MCW linearity was measured between 0.34 and 126.74 μGy.
evidence.evidenceText: A1417MCW * The linearity of detector was measured between 0.34 and 126.74μGy.

evidence.claim: F1417MCW linearity was measured between 0.27 and 121.5 μGy.
evidence.evidenceText: F1417MCW * The linearity of detector was measured between 0.27 and 121.5μGy.

evidence.claim: Actual lag measurements were 0.010 for A1717MCW, 0.011 for A1417MCW and 0.007 for F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Actual measurement of A1717MCW: 0.010 Actual measurement of A1417MCW: 0.011 Actual measurement of F1417MCW: 0.007

evidence.claim: The device does not provide under-scanning functionality.
evidence.evidenceText: This device does not provide the applicable functionality.

evidence.claim: The active area is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.
evidence.evidenceText: The active area of this device is 425.04 mm x 425.6 mm(A1717MCW), 345.24 mm x 425.6 mm(A1417MCW/F1417MCW) and is in full use and no under-scanning is possible.

evidence.claim: The X-ray system is designed to be installed with the detector input perpendicular to the X-ray beam axis.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray system is designed to be installed with the detector input perpendicular to the X-ray beam axis.

evidence.claim: After readout, at least 1 second is required and 7 seconds are needed to stabilize offset, making the minimum time between exposures 8 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Readout of the detector after an x-ray exposure requires least 1 second with window 0.5 second. And to stabilize offset 7 seconds are needed. Thus, the minimum time between exposures is 8 seconds.

evidence.claim: The device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.

evidence.claim: The report states that A1717MCW, A1417MCW and F1417MCW meet regulatory standards as outlined in safety report REP001824 and EMC reports NK-22-E-0811 and NK-22-E-0814.
evidence.evidenceText: 5.1 REGULATORY APPROVALSThe A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW meets the regulatory standards as outlined in the A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW safety report: REP001824, EMC report: NK-22-E-0811, NK-22-E-0814

evidence.claim: The exposure condition for line chart test pattern images was 75 kVp, 100 mA, 10 mAs, SID 100 cm.
evidence.evidenceText: Exposure Condition - 75kVp, 100mA, 10mAs, SID: 100cm

evidence.claim: Linearity and sensitivity measurements were reported as A1717MCW 127.10 LSB/μGy, A1417MCW 126.74 LSB/uGy and F1417MCW 121.5 LSB/uGy.
evidence.evidenceText: Linearity & Sensitivity * A1717MCW: 127.10 LSB/μGy ... * A1417MCW: 126.74 LSB/uGy ... * F1417MCW: 121.5 LSB/uGy
evidence.pageTarget: Digital X-ray Detector Linearity and Sensitivity

evidence.claim: MTF results were A1717MCW 84%, 65%, 30%, 13%; A1417MCW 85%, 65%, 33%, 17%; and F1417MCW 86%, 66%, 34%, 17% at the listed MTF points.
DQE results were A1717MCW 79%, 69%, 62%, 49%, 31%; A1417MCW 78%, 69%, 61%, 50%, 34%; and F1417MCW 76%, 71%, 64%, 51%, 35% at the listed DQE points.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal indicates MFDS/domestic, but the source text does not explicitly identify MFDS or a domestic authority.
The document references 21CFR1020.31 or .32 in a section prompt, but it does not explicitly state an issuing authority or jurisdiction.
Several mathematical expressions appear missing or degraded in the preprocessed text, including DQE formula content and noise model variables.
Some DQE lines contain likely OCR or formatting artifacts such as '3.0 lp/mm (0 ≥ 31%)', so those extracted values are lower confidence where taken from that section.

Approval date is Nov/21/2022 while revision date and prepared/review dates are Nov/18/2022; documentDate was set to the revision/preparation date because no single document effective date is explicitly labeled.
